clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: The DMU zone is located in the Downtown Periphery area surrounding much of the downtown core. The area supports a range of mixed uses including mixed groundfloor es like commercial, office and residential with a mix of uses on the upper floors. The lot fabric in these areas is generally narrow with less height.
Rezoning to the DN or DMS zone may be considered in the DMU Zone if the requirements of the Official Plan can be demonstrated to be met.
The following uses are permitted in the DMU Zone:
Uses Permitted if not within 400m of any Residential Neighbourhood Zone:

These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met: the front yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 40% of the streetline the flankage yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 50% of the streetline
Front Yard Setback min. 0 m max. 3 m
Flankage Yard Setback min. 0 m max. 6 m
Encroachments into the public street right- of-way for decks, signage, public art, and architectural features above 3 m from grade at the streetline may be considered by Planning Board.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.1.
Short term rentals are permitted following the requirements of Section 4.10.
Sidewalk cafes and patios may be permitted following the requirements of Section 4.2.
Developments lower than 6 m in height may be permitted by Development Agreement.
One building per lot is permitted.
The minimum building height shall be 3 storeys.
The maximum building height shall be:
For registered heritage properties, the height shall be the height of the existing heritage building unless negotiated through a Heritage Development Agreement.
The maximum streetwall height shall be 3 storeys.
The minimum streetwall stepback shall be 2.5 m.
The streetwall stepback may be less than the minimum requirement for up to 30% of the width of the building facing the streetline.
The maximum building dimension is 60 m.

Groundfloor uses in the DMU zone may range from any mix of commercial, office, institutional and residential uses. 2.5 m at 3rd Storey for 70% Min. Lot Area NA Front Yard Setback 0-3 m for up to 40% of streetline frontage Min. Lot Frontage 7.6 m
Parking garage entries are permitted if they are less than 40% of the frontage.
Parking garage doors shall be set back no less than 3 m from the front lot line.
For multi-unit buildings, each grade-related unit shall include a patio door.
